funds.Info 10 - About (Fund): that invests in all the companies that make up the Nifty 50 Index. This index represents India’s top 50 companies based on market capitalization. The fund allocates at least 95% of its total assets to these companies in the same proportion as the index, while the remaining 5% is invested in cash and money market instruments for liquidity purposes. The fund tracks the Nifty 50 TRIas its benchmark. This index reflects the overall performance of the 50 companies, including any dividends paid out. By mirroring this index, the fund aims to deliver returns that are closely aligned with the broader market performance. The fund managers do not try to outperform the index but rather aim to provide investors with a simple and efficient way to invest in India’s leading companies. Whenever there is a change in the composition of the index, the fund also rebalances its portfolio accordingly. Being a passively managed fund, it also benefits from a lower expense ratio, making it a cost-effective option for long-term investors.

funds.Info 10 - About (Fund): The fund allocates at least 80% of its assets to equity, preference shares and fully convertible debentures. The remaining is invested in money market instruments to maintain liquidity. The fund enjoys tax benefits under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act. Investors can reduce their tax liability by up to ₹46,800. Investments in the SBI long-term fund are spread across large, small and mid-cap stocks to ensure the portfolio is well diversified. The fund has a mandatory lock-in period of 3 years. Investment Strategy The fund is actively managed by Dinesh Balchandran, who follows a bottom-up stock-picking approach along with fundamental analysis. The investment process involves identifying companies with strong business models and sound management. Each stock in the portfolio is selected after a deep evaluation of its financial health. Who Should Invest In SBI Long Term Equity Fund - Direct Growth?You should invest in the SBI long-term fund if:-You are a first-time investor-You are looking for tax benefits-You want a diversified portfolio Taxation on SBI Long Term Fund Since equity-linked saving schemes have a lock-in period of 3 years, they are not subject to short-term capital gain (STCG). Any gains from selling the SBI long-term fund after 3 years are subject to long-term capital gain or LTCG at the rate of 12.5%. Allocation & Historical Returns

funds.Info 10 - About (Fund): is alarge-cap mutual fundthat invests in the top 100 companies ranked on the stock exchange by their market capitalization. The fund invests at least 80% of its assets inlarge-cap stocks. The remaining 20% is invested in REITs, debt instruments and money market instruments. SBI Bluechip Direct Growth Fund maintains a strong focus on industries like Finance, Automobile, FMCG, Technology, Energy, etc. The fund managers follow a mix of growth andvalue style investingto pick stocks for the bluechip fund. This includes picking stocks that are expected to grow faster than others and are undervalued.
